extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping back_pain from false to true (all other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity, where order is self_care < routine < urgent < emergent.

Rule r4 (priority 100) requires back_pain=true and sets acuity := self_care, the LOWEST value. Because it has the highest priority, activating it overrides the higher-acuity rules — this is the monotonicity-breaking pattern.

Counterexample comparison with confusion=true, vision_loss=true, chest_pain=false:

Baseline (back_pain=false): r1 matches -> urgent (prio 2); r2 matches -> emergent (prio 3); r3 no match; r4 no match (back_pain false). Highest-priority match is r2 -> acuity = emergent.

Witness (back_pain=true): r1 -> urgent (prio 2); r2 -> emergent (prio 3); r4 now matches (confusion AND vision_loss AND back_pain) -> self_care (prio 100). Highest-priority match is r4 -> acuity = self_care.

Setting back_pain true lowered acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating the property. The witness_assignment gives the full input where the violation manifests (compared against the same input with back_pain=false). All four input variables are specified.
